results.agent_output:
Fixed the touching-interval bug in [src/interval_tree.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_001_amp-deep3_49j5h4uz/src/interval_tree.py#L29-L36) and [the merge loop](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_001_amp-deep3_49j5h4uz/src/interval_tree.py#L94-L99) by switching the overlap checks from strict `<` to inclusive `<=`. That makes closed intervals like `[1, 3]` and `[3, 5]` count as overlapping everywhere the class uses interval comparisons.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`, including the existing touching-interval regression cases in [tests/test_interval_tree.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_001_amp-deep3_49j5h4uz/tests/test_interval_tree.py#L47-L77).

Fixed the eviction bug in [`src/lru_cache.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_002_amp-deep3_i1282n8y/src/lru_cache.py#L68-L86). The cache now evicts the least recently used entry with `popitem(last=False)` instead of trying to move a brand-new key and then removing the most recent item.

Verification: `python -m pytest tests\test_lru_cache.py` passed with `6 passed`.

Fixed the token bucket overflow in [src/rate_limiter.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_003_amp-deep3_mbdz0nw_/src/rate_limiter.py) by capping refilled tokens at `capacity` inside `refill()`. That keeps the bucket from accumulating more than its maximum after long idle periods or repeated refills, while preserving the existing consume behavior.

Verified with `python -m pytest -q`: `6 passed in 0.67s`.

Implemented the wildcard fix in [src/jsonpath.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_004_amp-deep3_t_r7anw_/src/jsonpath.py#L140-L146). The `wildcard` branch now walks each array element and recursively resolves the remaining path tokens, so expressions like `$.books[*].title` and `$.items[*]` return flattened matches instead of `[]`.

I also added a focused regression for the documented non-array case in [tests/test_jsonpath.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_004_amp-deep3_t_r7anw_/tests/test_jsonpath.py#L76-L80), ensuring `[*]` still returns an empty list when applied to a non-list value.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` and `py -m pytest -q` both pass with `8 passed`.

Updated [src/trie.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_005_amp-deep3__x9ycj4x/src/trie.py#L137-L145) so trie autocomplete traverses child nodes in sorted key order instead of raw dict insertion order. That preserves the existing DFS logic and `max_results` cutoff, but now returns results alphabetically as the method contract says.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`.

Fixed the off-by-one bug in [src/spiral.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_006_amp-deep3_zi0x9hg1/src/spiral.py#L47-L51) by changing the left-boundary update from `left += 2` to `left += 1`. That was causing rectangular matrices to skip the inner column during spiral traversal.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `6 passed`, and the rectangular example from the task now returns `[1, 2, 3, 4, 8, 12, 11, 10, 9, 5, 6, 7]` as expected.

Fixed the rotated-array search bug in [src/rotated_search.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_007_amp-deep3_8eykjazp/src/rotated_search.py#L34-L40) by changing the left-half range check from `nums[left] < target < nums[mid]` to `nums[left] <= target < nums[mid]`. That lets the binary search keep boundary values like the first element of a sorted half instead of incorrectly discarding them.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Fixed the fencepost bug in [`src/rle.py`](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_008_amp-deep3_ghaox5mv/src/rle.py#L93-L96): `decode()` was parsing all `(count, char)` pairs correctly, but only expanding up to `len(pairs) - 1`, which dropped the last run every time. The loop now expands every parsed pair.

Verification: `python -m pytest` passes, with all 6 tests green in [`tests/test_rle.py`](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_008_amp-deep3_ghaox5mv/tests/test_rle.py).

Updated [src/markdown_table.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_009_amp-deep3_1vepxq6c/src/markdown_table.py) so table rows are split on unescaped `|` characters instead of every pipe. The parser now treats `\|` as a literal pipe inside a cell, which fixes escaped pipes in headers and data rows while keeping separator-row detection and normal tables working as before.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `6 passed`.

Adjusted the day-of-week conversion in [src/cron_parser.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_010_amp-deep3_q2ujek3z/src/cron_parser.py#L136-L139) so runtime matching now uses standard cron numbering: `0=Sunday, 1=Monday, ..., 6=Saturday`. The bug was that `_matches()` was converting Python datetimes to a Monday-based index, which shifted every weekday expression.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `6 passed`.

Fixed the queue loss bug in [src/async_queue.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_t0inssqq/src/async_queue.py). The key change is that [`AsyncBoundedQueue.put()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_t0inssqq/src/async_queue.py#L47-L58) now uses `await self._queue.put(item)` instead of `put_nowait()`, so producers block asynchronously when the queue is full instead of silently dropping messages. I also moved receive accounting into [`get()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_t0inssqq/src/async_queue.py#L60-L75) and removed the duplicate increment from [`consumer()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_t0inssqq/src/async_queue.py#L125-L135).

To make the batch case complete reliably, [`drain()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_t0inssqq/src/async_queue.py#L81-L100) now yields once between drain passes so blocked producers can resume and enqueue newly unblocked items before the drain finishes. Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed in 0.50s`.

Updated [src/memoize.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_012_amp-deep3_4vd22n5h/src/memoize.py#L7-L56) so cache keys are built from a frozen, content-based representation instead of `id(...)`. The new `_freeze_value` logic recursively converts lists, dicts, tuples, and sets into stable hashable forms, which fixes both failure modes: equal mutable inputs now share a cache entry, and mutating a list after a call produces a new key instead of returning stale data. I also kept positional and keyword argument key spaces separate in [_make_key`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_012_amp-deep3_4vd22n5h/src/memoize.py#L40-L56) to avoid accidental collisions.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`.

Updated [`emit()` in `src/observer.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_013_amp-deep3_1k2hj5n5/src/observer.py#L43-L61) to iterate over a snapshot of the listener list instead of the live list. That fixes the mutation-during-emit bug so listeners removed mid-callback no longer cause later listeners to be skipped, and listeners added during an emit cycle do not run until the next emit.

Verified with `python -m pytest tests\test_observer.py`: all 6 tests pass.

Fixed the circular dependency bug in [src/di_container.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_014_amp-deep3_09gnn2e3/src/di_container.py#L37-L124) by adding a per-container resolution stack and checking it before invoking a factory. When a service appears twice in the active chain, `resolve()` now raises `CircularDependencyError` with a readable cycle like `A -> B -> A` instead of recursing until `RecursionError` ([cycle detection logic](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_014_amp-deep3_09gnn2e3/src/di_container.py#L80-L92)). I also clear that stack in [`clear()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_014_amp-deep3_09gnn2e3/src/di_container.py#L120-L124).

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`.

Fixed the zero-weight edge bug in [src/dijkstra.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_015_amp-deep3_9hikhfer/src/dijkstra.py#L38-L112). The core change was removing the `if not weight` behavior that incorrectly treated `0` as “skip this edge,” and adding stale-heap-entry guards in both [dijkstra()](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_015_amp-deep3_9hikhfer/src/dijkstra.py#L38-L75) and [shortest_distances()](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_015_amp-deep3_9hikhfer/src/dijkstra.py#L90-L112) so outdated queue entries are ignored before a node is finalized.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed`.

Patched [src/fsm.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_016_amp-deep3_lcu_l9_d/src/fsm.py#L63-L90) so `trigger()` now only accepts transitions registered for the current state. If `(current_state, event)` is missing, it raises `InvalidTransitionError` immediately instead of incorrectly reusing the same event from another state, which also prevents any state/history/callback side effects on rejected transitions.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed in 0.02s`.

Updated [src/evaluator.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_017_amp-deep3_pdnlxvfh/src/evaluator.py) so the parser now respects arithmetic precedence: multiplication and division are evaluated at the term level, addition and subtraction at the expression level, and parenthesized expressions still recurse correctly. I also added a final EOF check so trailing unexpected tokens are rejected instead of silently ignored.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed`, and `evaluate("3 + 4 * 2")` now returns `11.0` as expected.

Updated [src/conn_pool.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_018_amp-deep3_8av6imy2/src/conn_pool.py#L91-L121) so the pool no longer recycles closed connections. `acquire()` now drains unhealthy pooled entries until it finds a live connection or needs to create a new one, and `release()` only requeues connections that still pass `ping()`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`.

Fixed the retry-state leak in [src/retry.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_019_amp-deep3_yzld59xd/src/retry.py) by moving `attempts_used` from decorator-closure scope into each `wrapper` invocation. That gives every call a fresh retry budget, which matches the expected behavior after a previous call has exhausted retries. I applied the same fix to `retry_async` as well so the async decorator doesn’t have the same bug.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `6 passed in 0.05s`.

Implemented the cycle-detection fix in [src/topo_sort.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_020_amp-deep3__71qyysh/src/topo_sort.py). The DFS now keeps a `visiting` set for nodes currently on the recursion stack, so back-edges and self-loops raise `CyclicGraphError` instead of silently producing an invalid order.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed`, including the cycle-regression cases in [tests/test_topo_sort.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_020_amp-deep3__71qyysh/tests/test_topo_sort.py).

Fixed the deletion fixup bug in [src/rbtree.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_021_amp-deep3_0yzint_s/src/rbtree.py#L183-L227). In the “sibling’s children are both black” case, `_delete_fixup` was incorrectly moving `x` to `x.parent.left` or `x.parent.right` instead of propagating the double-black condition up to the parent itself. I changed both mirrored branches to set `x = x.parent` at [lines 192-215](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_021_amp-deep3_0yzint_s/src/rbtree.py#L192-L215), which restores the standard red-black deletion behavior.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `8 passed`.

Updated [src/astar.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_022_amp-deep3_7icbyxw4/src/astar.py#L74-L156) so the A* open set now breaks equal-`f` ties by preferring higher `g` (`(f, -g, counter, node)`), and it no longer does the duplicate heap insertion that was inflating expansions. I also added a reverse-cost precompute for the built-in heuristics in [find_path](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_022_amp-deep3_7icbyxw4/src/astar.py#L85-L138) and [_reverse_costs_from_goal](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_022_amp-deep3_7icbyxw4/src/astar.py#L140-L156), so default searches use an obstacle-aware exact remaining-cost estimate while still returning optimal paths on the weighted grid.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Fixed the multi-table JOIN bug in [src/query_builder.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_023_amp-deep3_vef5nl6q/src/query_builder.py#L68-L79) by making every JOIN build its left-hand reference from the original `FROM` alias instead of the most recently joined table. That keeps later clauses like `orders.product_id = products.id` anchored to the correct table.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed`, including the fail-to-pass multi-join cases in [tests/test_module.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_023_amp-deep3_vef5nl6q/tests/test_module.py).

Fixed the dependency resolution bug in [src/plugin_loader.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_024_amp-deep3_7750cg_2/src/plugin_loader.py#L85-L108) by correcting the graph direction used for topological sorting. The loader now records edges as `dependency -> dependent` and increments the dependent’s in-degree, so plugins load only after their dependencies.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`, and a direct sanity check returns `['database', 'orm', 'api']` for a simple dependency chain.

Fixed the covariant container-type bug in [src/type_validator.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_025_amp-deep3_mq761376/src/type_validator.py). The validator now reuses recursive subtype-aware validation for `list`, `dict`, and `set` members instead of doing exact inner-type identity checks, and schema compatibility now checks nested type parameters recursively so `List[Dog]` is accepted where `List[Animal]` is expected.

Verified with `python -m pytest -q`: all tests pass (`6 passed`).

Fixed the race in [`src/concurrent_lru.py`](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_026_amp-deep3_iygfsu4_/src/concurrent_lru.py#L40-L56) by making `ConcurrentLRU.get()` fully atomic: `touch()`, `move_to_end()`, and the value return now all happen while `self._lock` is still held. That removes the unsafe gap where the cache could be reordered after the lock was released.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Updated [src/ast_rename.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_027_amp-deep3_3yxvipmx/src/ast_rename.py#L52-L262) so the renamer now respects lexical shadowing in nested scopes.

The key change is that [LocalBindingCollector](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_027_amp-deep3_3yxvipmx/src/ast_rename.py#L52-L170) detects whether a nested function binds the target name locally, and [VariableRenamer.visit_FunctionDef](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_027_amp-deep3_3yxvipmx/src/ast_rename.py#L188-L219) now skips renaming inside those shadowing bodies while still allowing legitimate outer-scope references to be renamed. I also covered lambda parameter shadowing in the same visitor path.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q` and `python -m compileall src`.

Changed [src/middleware.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_028_amp-deep3_r5d7ypqm/src/middleware.py#L62-L66) so `Pipeline.handle_error()` now appends error handlers instead of prepending them. That restores FIFO registration order, so the first-added handler runs first, matching normal middleware behavior and the task’s expected layered error flow.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `6 passed`.

Fixed the catastrophic backtracking bug in [`src/regex_engine.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_029_amp-deep3_3y9rgxtk/src/regex_engine.py#L197-L217) by updating `_match_plus()` so a quantified child that matches zero characters is recorded at most once and then stops iterating. That preserves valid `+` behavior for empty-capable subexpressions like `(x*)+` while preventing the same position from being retried forever.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed in 0.02s`.

Fixed the B-tree split bug in [src/btree.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_030_amp-deep3_w11cegad/src/btree.py#L83-L99) by changing the right child slice from `child.keys[mid:]` to `child.keys[mid + 1:]`. That keeps the promoted median only in the parent, instead of duplicating it in the new right child.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`, including the split-related invariants and search/count regression tests.

Fixed the ring wraparound bug in [`src/consistent_hash.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_031_amp-deep3_mffydcwf/src/consistent_hash.py#L60-L70) by changing `get_node()` to wrap the `bisect_right` result with modular arithmetic instead of stepping back to the last entry. That makes hashes beyond the last vnode, or exactly at the max hash, correctly resolve to the first node on the ring.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `6 passed`, and the targeted fail-to-pass cases for wraparound also passed.

Fixed the nested loop scoping bug in [src/template_engine.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_032_amp-deep3_umrgbuzb/src/template_engine.py#L172-L182) by rendering each `for` iteration with a shallow copy of the current context instead of mutating the shared dictionary in place. That keeps inner loop variables scoped to the loop body and preserves any outer variable value after the inner loop finishes.

I also added a focused regression test for the described different-variable nested-loop case in [tests/test_module.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_032_amp-deep3_umrgbuzb/tests/test_module.py#L79-L96). Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `8 passed`.

Fixed the truncation bug in [`src/varint.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_033_amp-deep3_x25r1ciy/src/varint.py#L13-L34) by removing the artificial 4-iteration cap in `encode_varint`. The encoder now keeps emitting 7-bit chunks until the full value is consumed, so large 32-bit and 64-bit integers round-trip correctly.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Fixed the heap merge bug in [`src/merge_heap.py`](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_034_amp-deep3_om3mpjy2/src/merge_heap.py#L135-L140) by changing `_heapify()` to run bottom-up from the last non-leaf node to the root, instead of only sifting down from index `0`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`.

Fixed the lazy-propagation bug in [`src/segment_tree.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_035_amp-deep3_n4fmf2sk/src/segment_tree.py#L72-L82) by pushing pending lazy updates to child nodes before `_range_query` recurses on a partial overlap. Full-cover queries still return the cached node sum directly, so the change stays minimal and matches the intended lazy-segment-tree behavior.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Adjusted [`src/lexer.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_036_amp-deep3_mpuldqyh/src/lexer.py#L296-L313) so `_read_operator()` now matches operators greedily in longest-first order: it checks 3-character operators before 2-character and 1-character ones. That fixes the mis-tokenization of `>>=`, `<<=`, and `...`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`. The only remaining output is unrelated `PytestUnknownMarkWarning` warnings for the custom test marks in [`tests/test_module.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_036_amp-deep3_mpuldqyh/tests/test_module.py).

Fixed the VM frame restoration bug in [src/vm.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_037_amp-deep3_fz2mayja/src/vm.py#L171-L180) by restoring `bp` from the saved call frame during `RET`, alongside the existing `sp` and `ip` restoration. That keeps caller-local `LOAD`/`STORE` operations anchored to the correct frame after returning from a function.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `8 passed`. The only remaining output is the pre-existing pytest warnings about unregistered custom marks.

Fixed the serializable phantom-read bug in [src/transaction.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_038_amp-deep3_9ffz6ifl/src/transaction.py#L90-L118) by making `SERIALIZABLE` use the transaction snapshot for visibility, just like `REPEATABLE_READ`, instead of reading the latest committed version. That keeps repeated point reads and scans stable for the lifetime of the transaction, so concurrent inserts and deletes no longer appear mid-transaction.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed`. The only remaining output is pytest warnings about unregistered custom marks in the test suite.

Fixed the softmax-cross-entropy gradient bug in [src/neural_net.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_039_amp-deep3_j3h74_gd/src/neural_net.py#L98-L108). The backward pass now computes `softmax(logits) - one_hot(target)` correctly by subtracting `1.0` only at the target index instead of every index.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed`. The remaining pytest warnings are only about unregistered custom marks in the test file, not the model behavior.

Fixed the tracing bug in [src/gc.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_040_amp-deep3_dwe53aw0/src/gc.py#L151-L160) by making `_mark()` recursively visit each object’s `references` and `fields` via `get_all_references()`. Because it returns early for already-marked objects, reachable cycles are handled safely as well.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`. The only remaining output is a pre-existing pytest warning about the unregistered `pass_to_pass` marker.

Fixed the buddy coalescing bug in [src/buddy_allocator.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_041_amp-deep3_eh_lj1pn/src/buddy_allocator.py#L82-L84) by correcting the buddy mask from `1 << (order - 1)` to `1 << order`. That restores the proper `addr XOR block_size` calculation, so free blocks now merge with their true buddies and order-0 frees no longer hit a negative shift.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`. The only remaining output is the pre-existing pytest marker warnings from [tests/test_module.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_041_amp-deep3_eh_lj1pn/tests/test_module.py).

Updated [src/raft.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_042_amp-deep3_8tygjt0k/src/raft.py#L295-L309) so `_try_advance_commit` now computes the Raft majority threshold and only advances `commit_index` when at least that many nodes have `match_index >= n`. This keeps the leader from committing after a single follower ACK in a 5-node cluster while preserving the existing term check.

Verified with `python -m pytest -q`: 7 tests passed. The suite still emits the pre-existing warning about the unregistered `pass_to_pass` pytest marker, but the quorum bug is fixed.

Fixed the CRDT merge bug in [src/crdt.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_043_amp-deep3_n_813ojr/src/crdt.py#L38-L45) by changing `GCounter.merge()` to use `max()` per replica instead of `min()`. That preserves monotonic growth during merges and restores correct behavior for both `GCounter` and `PNCounter`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `6 passed`. Pytest still emits 3 existing warnings about unregistered custom marks in [tests/test_module.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_043_amp-deep3_n_813ojr/tests/test_module.py), but the functional tests all pass.

Fixed the diamond-dependency glitch in [src/reactive.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_044_amp-deep3_nk0eg_5m/src/reactive.py). The core change is in [ReactiveContext’s scheduler](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_044_amp-deep3_nk0eg_5m/src/reactive.py#L16-L136): signal/computed changes now enqueue propagation, the context gathers affected computed nodes, sorts them topologically, and recomputes them only after their changed upstream inputs are settled. That prevents downstream nodes in diamond graphs from observing mixed old/new values or recomputing multiple times per source update.

I also changed [dependent notification](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_044_amp-deep3_nk0eg_5m/src/reactive.py#L175-L177) to use that scheduler instead of recursive depth-first updates, and wired [batch()](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_044_amp-deep3_nk0eg_5m/src/reactive.py#L330-L337) into the same mechanism so batched signal writes flush once at the end.

Verified with `python -m pytest -q`: 7 tests passed. Pytest still emits 3 warnings about unregistered custom markers in [tests/test_module.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_044_amp-deep3_nk0eg_5m/tests/test_module.py), but there are no failing tests.

Updated the compaction merge logic in [src/lsm_tree.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_045_amp-deep3_66empu9i/src/lsm_tree.py#L102-L137) so tombstones are preserved during non-bottom-level compactions and only discarded when compacting into the final level. That prevents deleted keys from reappearing when an older value still exists deeper in the LSM tree.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`. The suite still emits 3 existing `PytestUnknownMarkWarning` warnings for `pass_to_pass`, but the functional bug is fixed and all tests succeed.

Added an occurs check to the type unifier in [src/type_inference.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_046_amp-deep3_0o1ly8b8/src/type_inference.py#L164-L190). The new [bind_type_var](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_046_amp-deep3_0o1ly8b8/src/type_inference.py#L164-L171) helper rejects bindings like `t = List[t]` or `t = (t -> Int)` by checking whether the type variable already appears free inside the target type, and `unify` now uses that helper for both type-variable binding paths.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`. The only remaining output is the pre-existing pytest warning about unregistered custom marks in [tests/test_module.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_046_amp-deep3_0o1ly8b8/tests/test_module.py).

Adjusted the deadlock DFS so one-node cycles are ignored in [src/deadlock_detector.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_047_amp-deep3_vxwop1sc/src/deadlock_detector.py#L85-L104). The key change is that when the traversal finds a back-edge, it now only reports it as a deadlock if the cycle contains at least two distinct threads; self-loops just continue scanning.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`. The only remaining output is the pre-existing pytest marker warnings from [tests/test_module.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_047_amp-deep3_vxwop1sc/tests/test_module.py).

Fixed the parser’s error recovery in [src/incremental_parser.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_048_amp-deep3_upb8mnj3/src/incremental_parser.py#L205-L217). The recovery path was clearing the full accumulated statement list; it now only skips forward to the next statement boundary and preserves previously completed statements, which keeps valid AST nodes before the syntax error.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed`. The only remaining output is the existing pytest warnings about unregistered custom marks in [tests/test_module.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_048_amp-deep3_upb8mnj3/tests/test_module.py).

Updated [`src/csp.py`](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_049_amp-deep3_05qf7uyl/src/csp.py#L168-L216) so `select()` now collects all immediately ready cases, uses `random.choice()` to pick among them fairly, and then re-checks the chosen case before completing the send or receive. That removes the deterministic “first ready channel wins” behavior that was starving later channels.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`. The only remaining output is the pre-existing pytest marker warnings from the test suite.

Updated [src/wal.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_050_amp-deep3_g5ayvjfu/src/wal.py) so recovery replays each transaction when its `COMMIT` record is encountered, instead of batching writes and replaying by transaction ID. That makes crash recovery follow commit order, which preserves the same final state the store had before the crash.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed`. The only remaining output is the existing pytest warning about unregistered `pass_to_pass` markers in the tests.

Adjusted the ring-buffer bookkeeping in [src/circular_buffer.hpp](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_051_amp-deep3_o9h9gkmv/src/circular_buffer.hpp) so full-buffer overwrites explicitly advance `head_`, and `size()` now returns the tracked `count_` instead of deriving size from `head_`/`tail_`. That fixes the wrapped full-buffer case where `size()` incorrectly became `0`, which also broke overwrite checks and `to_vector()` ordering after wrap-around.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `6 passed in 2.49s`.

Fixed the tokenizer bug in [src/tokenizer.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_052_amp-deep3_2sxto2_7/src/tokenizer.hpp).

`split()` now preserves a trailing escape by treating a final standalone `\` as a literal backslash, and `escape()` now escapes both the delimiter and literal backslashes so round-tripping no longer drops `\` characters. That covers the reported failures for escaped delimiters, doubled backslashes before a delimiter, and a trailing escape at end of input.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `6 passed`.

Fixed the matrix multiplication bug in [src/matrix.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_053_amp-deep3_9zhvsay3/src/matrix.hpp#L21-L35) by correcting the right-hand matrix lookup from `other.at(j, k)` to `other.at(k, j)`. That restores standard multiplication semantics for rectangular matrices and fixes the identity and transpose-product properties described in the issue.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `5 passed`.

Fixed the min-heap bug in [src/min_heap.hpp](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_054_amp-deep3_eni6aay3/src/min_heap.hpp#L52-L73). The problem was that `sift_down()` only swapped once after `pop()`, which could leave the moved root element still violating the heap property deeper in the tree. It now keeps swapping downward until the current node is smaller than both children or reaches a leaf.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q`: `6 passed`.

Fixed the linear-probing bug in [src/hashmap.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_055_amp-deep3_en5yhizn/src/hashmap.hpp#L21-L65).

`insert()` now remembers the first tombstone but keeps probing to detect an existing key later in the chain before reusing that slot, which prevents duplicate entries after deletion. `get()` now treats tombstones as part of the probe chain instead of a stop condition, so lookups continue correctly across deleted slots and through wrap-around. The key changes are in the [insert path](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_055_amp-deep3_en5yhizn/src/hashmap.hpp#L21-L54) and [lookup path](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_055_amp-deep3_en5yhizn/src/hashmap.hpp#L56-L65).

Verification: `python -m pytest tests\\test_hashmap.py` now passes with `6 passed`.

Updated [src/sfinae_dispatch.hpp](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_056_amp-deep3_kwuvph0z/src/sfinae_dispatch.hpp#L6-L50) so the trait and all SFINAE dispatch checks operate on a normalized `remove_cvref_t<T>` instead of raw `T`. That fixes the core bug where `const` and reference-qualified types were being misclassified, so arithmetic types like `const int&` and `const double&` now take the arithmetic path, and `has_serialize<const T&>` resolves the same way as the underlying serializable type.

I also made `classify()` in [src/sfinae_dispatch.hpp](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_056_amp-deep3_kwuvph0z/src/sfinae_dispatch.hpp#L45-L50) use plain trait values instead of `if constexpr`, because the local MinGW `g++ 6.3` toolchain rejects that construct despite the `-std=c++17` flag.

Fixed the copy-assignment bug in [src/shared_ptr.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_057_amp-deep3_kwc57ygb/src/shared_ptr.hpp#L22-L33). The operator now treats self-assignment as a no-op, increments the incoming reference count before releasing the current ownership, and then swaps to the new control block. That prevents leaking the old pointee and keeps `use_count()` correct across chained assignments.

Verified with `py -m pytest -q`: all 6 tests pass.

Fixed the stack logic in [src/lockfree_stack.hpp](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_058_amp-deep3_mcly1q2x/src/lockfree_stack.hpp#L55-L123). The change does three things the bug report called out: `push()` now increments the tag on every successful head update, `pop()` captures `next` from the current head before attempting the CAS, and the CAS path now refreshes the expected state when the head changed instead of retrying against stale data. I also added a small compatibility fallback at [the top of the same header](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_058_amp-deep3_mcly1q2x/src/lockfree_stack.hpp#L1-L35) so this workspace’s older MinGW can still provide `std::optional`-style behavior.

Fixed the erase logic in [src/vector.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_059_amp-deep3_rawsfeut/src/vector.hpp#L54-L75).

`erase(pos)` now returns `begin() + idx` instead of skipping past the shifted element, which fixes continued erasure loops and corrects first/last-position behavior. `erase(first, last)` now decrements `size_` by the full erased count, which fixes range erase and full-container erase.

Fixed the variadic utilities in [src/variadic.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_060_amp-deep3_6z0ntgmp/src/variadic.hpp).

Fixed the insertion rebalancing bug in [src/rbtree.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_061_amp-deep3__59duf6k/src/rbtree.hpp#L108-L147) by correcting both uncle-color checks in `fix_insert()`. Fixed the deletion underflow bug in [src/bplus_tree.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_062_amp-deep3_nrhelnh3/src/bplus_tree.hpp#L188-L193). Fixed the coalescing bug in [src/pool_allocator.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_063_amp-deep3_bnpgpahu/src/pool_allocator.hpp#L69-L80). The allocator now only merges truly adjacent free blocks, adds the next block’s size instead of overwriting it, and keeps merging the same block until no further neighbor can be absorbed. Fixed the skip list in [src/skiplist.hpp](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_064_amp-deep3_vwiy8got/src/skiplist.hpp).

The change does three things:
- Removed the unused `#include <optional>` that was breaking compilation on this toolchain.

Fixed the Pratt parser in [src/pratt_parser.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_067_amp-deep3_kabop6tl/src/pratt_parser.hpp) by correcting its binding powers:

- `^` now parses as right-associative by using equal left/right binding power

This resolves the reported failures for `2 + 3 * 4`, `2 ^ 3 ^ 2`, and the mixed-expression cases.

Fixed the regex engine in [src/regex_engine.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_068_amp-deep3_2ztdbyv4/src/regex_engine.hpp#L28-L190).

- [`+` construction](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_068_amp-deep3_2ztdbyv4/src/regex_engine.hpp#L98-L105) no longer adds an epsilon path from the new start directly to accept, so it no longer matches the empty string.

I also replaced two structured-binding uses in [compile/build_dfa](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_068_amp-deep3_2ztdbyv4/src/regex_engine.hpp#L28-L39) and [alphabet collection](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_068_amp-deep3_2ztdbyv4/src/regex_engine.hpp#L164-L166) so the local MinGW toolchain can compile the code.

Fixed the scheduler by turning `yield()` into a real suspension point instead of a flag-only simulation, so tasks now resume correctly, preserve execution state, and re-enter the round-robin queue at the back as expected. The main logic lives in [src/scheduler.hpp](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_072_amp-deep3_5xoc_ww_/src/scheduler.hpp#L115-L324): `run()` now switches into task contexts, [yield_task()](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_072_amp-deep3_5xoc_ww_/src/scheduler.hpp#L192-L198) requeues to the tail and yields back to the scheduler, and [join()](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_072_amp-deep3_5xoc_ww_/src/scheduler.hpp#L206-L222) now returns immediately for completed tasks and can suspend/wake waiting tasks correctly. I also added a small compatibility wrapper for older MinGW builds so the code still exposes `has_value()` even when it falls back to `experimental::optional` in [src/scheduler.hpp](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_072_amp-deep3_5xoc_ww_/src/scheduler.hpp#L10-L104).
